query: I definitely don’t want to change my plan, but I’m willing to refuel 2GB of data. Please go ahead and add 2GB so I can get back to excellent internet speed.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user reports mobile data that keeps stopping or slowing down and wants excellent speed.
assistant: The assistant authenticates the telecom account, identifies the affected line 555-123-2002, and checks the line plus plan details.
assistant: The assistant finds that the line has used 15.1 GB against a 15.0 GB data limit and explains that exceeding the limit is the visible cause of the slowdown.
user: The user declines a plan change and explicitly authorizes a 2GB data refuel.

candidate_answers.text: Understood. I’ll add 2GB of refuel data to your line now so we can restore service and then verify whether the connection improves.
The data cap overage has already been established from visible account state, and the user has explicitly approved the add-on, so refueling data is the most grounded next action.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user turn where the quota issue has already been established and the user explicitly selects a supported remediation rather than escalation or plan change.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Highest score goes to the single telecom action that directly remedies quota exhaustion under the user’s chosen constraint.
notes: The query is taken verbatim from the visible user turn before any tool execution.
This sample diversifies telecom best_action types by using refuel_data instead of state inspection or escalation.
